headline: Sarawak Smallholders Income Falls 40% on Palm Oil Crash
context: Sarawak 85,000 oil palm smallholders saw income drop from RM3,200 to RM1,920 monthly in 2025 following global price declines.

cards.big: 85,000 Sarawak smallholders lost 40% income as palm oil prices fell
cards.sub: Average monthly earnings dropped from RM3,200 to RM1,920 in 2025

cards.big: CPO price declined from RM4,200 to RM2,800 per tonne between 2024 and 2025
cards.sub: EU deforestation regulations reduced demand cutting Sarawak exports by 18%

cards.big: 72% of smallholders lack MSPO certification required for EU market access
cards.sub: Certification costs of RM8,000 per smallholder prohibitive without subsidies

cards.big: State launched RM150 million palm oil support fund in December 2025
cards.sub: Fund provides RM200 monthly supplement but covers only 60% of registered growers

cards.big: Price crashes test whether smallholders bear commodity risk alone or share it with industry
40% income drop for smallholders when palm oil crashes. Monoculture is Sarawak's structural vulnerability.
